alt: Sepia-toned 1900s-era photograph showing three Franciscan friars working in the colonial library of the Convent of San Francisco in Lima — one seated reading a folio in the foreground, one at the bookcases mid-frame, and one on a tall wooden ladder reaching the upper shelves
description: Stereograph card (number V21925) by the Keystone View Company showing three Franciscan friars at work in the colonial library of the Convento de San Francisco in Lima, Peru — one of the most important early libraries in the Americas, with shelves of leather-bound 16th- and 17th-century volumes. One side of the stereoscopic pair shown; original is reproduction 1s35135 in the LoC stereo collection.
photographer: Keystone View Company, publisher; Library of Congress Prints & Photographs Division (stereo collection)
title: Library of the San Francisco Convent, Lima, Peru
